Abide_By.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (abide_by, follow, heed, keep_to, adhere_to). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He followed the tenets of Buddhism.".
Abide_By.example: He followed the tenets of Buddhism.

Accept.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (accept, buy, understand, encourage). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I accepted how you did it.".
Accept.example: I accepted how you did it.

Acquiesce.definition: Agent acts upon Pivot, Theme (defer, accede, bow, capitulate, fall). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The enemy soldiers yielded.".

Act.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality (action, carry_out, carry_through, conduct, execute). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They carried out the deed.".

Addict.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Theme (addict, bias, dispose, incline, predispose). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I addicted him to Douglas Adams.".
Addict.example: I addicted him to Douglas Adams.

Adjust.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Goal, Source (adapt, accommodate, conform, adjust, gear). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He adapted himself.".

Admit.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Location (allow, let, hail, admit, include). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She admitted us.".

Adopt.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (assume, adopt, take, take_on). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He assumed the position that dogs are people.".
Adopt.example: He assumed the position that dogs are people.

Allow.definition: Causer acts upon Beneficiary, Eventuality (approve, authorize, endorse, okay, sanction). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "They tolerated birds.".

Amalgamate.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Co-Patient (intermarry, couple, incorporate, integrate, match). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The folk songs alternated well with pop songs.".
Amalgamate.example: The folk songs alternated well with pop songs.

Avoid.definition: Agent acts upon Location, Theme (avoid, boycott, circumvent, dodge, duck). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "We avoided the area.".

Become.definition: Patient acts upon Result (come_out, get, turn, turn_up, come). The grammatical subject is the patient. Example: "The belt came undone.".

Begin.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality, Subeventuality (go_on, proceed, set_out, set_about, begin). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He began to pack.".

Benefit.definition: Causer acts upon Beneficiary (benefit, profit). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "Sunlight benefited plants.".

Berry.definition: Agent acts upon Location (antique, berry, birdnest, blackberry, clam). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The children berried in the summer.".
Berry.example: The children berried in the summer.

Body_Motion.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal, Path (hold, extend, reach, stretch). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She reached her hand out.".

Calibratable_Cos.definition: Patient acts upon Attribute, Extent, Initial_State (build, die, appreciate, climb, decline). The grammatical subject is the patient. Example: "The price of oil soared.".

Care.definition: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (mind, care, worry, wonder). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "He didn't care.".

Caring.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (babysit, look_after, manage, mind, wait_on). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Jared looked after the children.".

Caused_Calibratable_Cos.definition: Causer acts upon Patient, Extent, Initial_State (commute, cut, raise, reduce, advance). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "Lawmakers cut the budget by 10% from $1.46 million to $1.3 million.".
Caused_Calibratable_Cos.example: Lawmakers cut the budget by 10% from $1.46 million to $1.3 million.

Complete.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality (accomplish, achieve, bring-off, complete, pull-off). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Wilma completed the assignment.".

Comprise.definition: Theme acts upon Pivot (comprise, compose, make, make_up, constitute). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "Textiles make up the majority of exports.".
Comprise.example: Textiles make up the majority of exports.

Conduct.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Beneficiary (conduct, give, head, hold, host). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The White House held a gala.".
Conduct.example: The White House held a gala.

Conspire.definition: Agent acts upon Co-Agent, Theme, Maleficiary (ally, collaborate, collude, conspire, machinate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "John and Sarah conspired.".

Continue.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality, Subeventuality (continue). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He continued to pack.".

Convert.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Initial_State, Result (advert, come_around, fall, get, get_around). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He converted to believing in Buddha.".
Convert.example: He converted to believing in Buddha.

Cope.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (deal, cope, manage, grapple, get_by). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He managed with climbing the slope.".
Cope.example: He managed with climbing the slope.

Dedicate.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal (dedicate, devote, commit). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I dedicated myself to helping us.".
Dedicate.example: I dedicated myself to helping us.

Delay.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality (postpone, delay, defer, stall, procrastinate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He delayed the watering.".

Discourage.definition: Causer acts upon Eventuality, Beneficiary (dissuade, deter, discourage, hamper, hinder). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "The sign discouraged us from smoking.".
Discourage.example: The sign discouraged us from smoking.

Disfunction.definition: Theme engages in the named event (die, go_down, go_off, go_out, poop_out). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "My phone died.".

Empathize.definition: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (empathize, sympathize, identify, commiserate). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "I empathized.".

Enforce.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (control, enforce, impose). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They enforced the rules.".

Establish.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Instrument (bring, constitute, constitutionalize, innovate, pioneer). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Zellig Harris established Penn's linguistics department.".
Establish.example: Zellig Harris established Penn's linguistics department.

Exchange.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Co-Theme, Co-Agent (barter, change, exchange, swap, trade). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Gwen exchanged the dress for a shirt.".
Exchange.example: Gwen exchanged the dress for a shirt.

Exclude.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal (omit, exclude, preclude). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They excluded me from the meeting.".
Exclude.example: They excluded me from the meeting.

Exhale.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (defecate, exhale, hemorrhage, perspire, expectorate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Paul exhaled.".

Flinch.definition: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (balk, cower, cringe, flinch, quail). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "Sharon flinched.".

Focus.definition: Agent acts upon Topic (focus, focalize, center, concentrate, contemplate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "We focused on it.".

Forbid.definition: Causer acts upon Eventuality, Beneficiary (keep, ban, bar, block, exclude). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "The rules forbade us from smoking.".
Forbid.example: The rules forbade us from smoking.

Free.definition: Causer acts upon Source, Theme (alleviate, liberate, discharge, exonerate, unburden). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "It freed him of guilt.".

Function.definition: Theme acts upon Attribute (go, go_on, run, perform, function). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "The old dishwasher still functioned.".

Help.definition: Agent acts upon Beneficiary, Theme (support, succor, aid, abet, assist). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I helped him.".

Hold.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (wield, handle, clasp, clutch, grab). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She held the rail.".

Involve.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal (involve, engage, relate, include, enroll). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They thoughtlessly involved me.".

Keep.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Location (hoard, hold, keep, leave, store). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Michelle kept the papers in the desk.".
Keep.example: Michelle kept the papers in the desk.

Let.definition: Agent acts upon Beneficiary, Eventuality (let). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They let us smoke.".

Limit.definition: Causer acts upon Patient, Goal (confine, limit, reduce, restrict, restrain). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "The lack of money limited the help.".
Limit.example: The lack of money limited the help.

Linger.definition: Agent acts upon Location, Eventuality (dally, dawdle, dither, equivocate, hesitate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Sasha dawdled in the museum.".

Matter.definition: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (matter, count). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "It matters.".

Neglect.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (disregard, forgo, forego, ignore, leave_out). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I disregarded the message.".

Orbit.definition: Theme acts upon Axis (orbit, revolve, rotate, twirl). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "The Earth revolved around the Sun.".
Orbit.example: The Earth revolved around the Sun.

Pain.definition: Experiencer acts upon Patient, Stimulus (ail, bother, burn, hurt, itch). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "My eyes were itching.".

Patent.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Result (copyright, patent, trademark, license, register). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I patented my idea.".

Play.definition: Agent acts upon Co-Agent, Theme (frolic, recreate, play). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Mary played.".

Promote.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (promote, advance, boost, further, encourage). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "We promoted the cause.".

Prosecute.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Theme, Attribute (book, bust, collar, impeach, nab). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They collared the suspect.".

Rebel.definition: Agent acts upon Maleficiary, Theme (discriminate, rebel, retaliate, sin, legislate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "John rebelled.".

Refrain.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality (refrain, abstain, desist, forbear). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He refrained.".

Rely.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (rely, depend, bank, bargain, count). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She relies on coming back in time.".
Rely.example: She relies on coming back in time.

Resign.definition: Agent acts upon Source, Attribute (leave_office, step_down, walk_out, resign, retire). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He resigned.".

Risk.definition: Agent acts upon Pivot, Theme, Eventuality (bet, gamble, punt, venture, wager). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I risked my job.".

Rotate.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Extent, Trajectory (curl, coil, hook, rotate, spin). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The camera rotated 90 degrees.".

Rush.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Eventuality (hasten, hurry, rush). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Maggie hurried with her lunch.".

Seem.definition: Theme acts upon Attribute (smell, sound, taste, keep, act). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "He appeared crazy.".

Shake.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Co-Patient (lump, mass, beat, fuse, scramble). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The sticks bundled easily.".

Spend_Time.definition: Agent acts upon Duration, Attribute (kill, lead, pass, serve, misspend). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I misspent 5 hours.".

Stop.definition: Affector acts upon Eventuality, Subeventuality (cut, discontinue, kill, close, cut_off). The grammatical subject is the affector. Example: "John cut the engine.".

Subjugate.definition: Agent acts upon Patient, Instrument (disenfranchise, enslave, mob, nourish, suppress). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Winnie the Pooh subjugated the unfortunate Pixies.".
Subjugate.example: Winnie the Pooh subjugated the unfortunate Pixies.

Subordinate.definition: Agent acts upon Co-Agent (answer, report). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Kevin reported to Martha.".

Succeed.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality (succeed, excel, manage, win, fall). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I succeeded.".

Suspect.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Attribute (suspect, accuse, condemn). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I suspected him of infidelity.".

Sustain.definition: Agent acts upon Theme, Instrument, Attribute (hold, maintain, sustain, prolong, protract). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I maintained a strong government through ruthless domination.".
Sustain.example: I maintained a strong government through ruthless domination.

Terminus.definition: Theme acts upon Location (begin, end, lead, start, stop). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "His property begins at the fence.".
Terminus.example: His property begins at the fence.

Trifle.definition: Agent acts upon Theme (dally, diddle, fiddle, play, toy). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She trifled with him.".

Try.definition: Agent acts upon Eventuality (try, attempt). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I tried apologizing.".

Void.definition: Agent acts upon Patient (annul, quash, void, avoid, invalidate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I nullified their advantage.".

Volunteer.definition: Agent acts upon Pivot, Theme, Attribute (offer, enlist, hire_on, sign_on, sign_up). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "John volunteered my sister for the task.".
Volunteer.example: John volunteered my sister for the task.

Weekend.definition: Theme acts upon Location (december, holiday, honeymoon, junket, overnight). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "My family always summered at the seashore.".
Weekend.example: My family always summered at the seashore.

Withdraw.definition: Agent acts upon Source (back_out, chicken_out, get_out, wriggle_out, bow_out). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He withdrew.".

Work.definition: Agent acts upon Co-Agent, Theme (bang_away, labor, labour, plug_away, slog_away). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Amanda worked.".
